    "project_recommendation": {
        "project_title": "Zero-ETL CDC Pipeline for Sub-Second Financial Analytics",
        "objective": "This project will demonstrate proficiency in building a data pipeline for real-time financial insights, bridging the gap between academic research and practical application within a production setting.",
        "tech_stack": [
            "Apache Spark 3.0",
            "Kafka",
            "Delta Lake",
            "Airflow"
        ],
        "implementation_steps": [
            "Phase 1: Design a schema for the financial data ingested by Spark. Utilize Delta Lake’s schema evolution capabilities to accommodate new data sources and transformations.",
            "Phase 2: Implement a Kafka consumer group to ingest the data stream. Utilize Spark Streaming or Structured Streaming for real-time data processing.",
            "Phase 3: Develop a data transformation pipeline within Spark using UDFs to clean and aggregate data, incorporating feature engineering for sentiment analysis and financial trends.",
            "Phase 4: Deploy the pipeline to a cloud platform (AWS or GCP) utilizing Airflow for orchestration, incorporating monitoring using Prometheus and Grafana.",
            "Phase 5: Create an API endpoint to serve the transformed data for downstream analytical use cases."
        ]
    }
}

    "project_recommendation": {
        "project_title": "Predictive Portfolio Allocation – Dynamic Network Modeling",
        "objective": "This project leverages the student's analytical skills to develop a dynamic portfolio allocation model utilizing graph theory and network analysis, significantly enhancing the quantitative researcher’s ability to predict market movements based on investor relationships and sentiment data.  It’s a direct application of the student's interest in finance and data mining.",
        "tech_stack": [
            "Apache Spark",
            "Graph Database (Neo4j)",
            "Python (PySpark)",

        "implementation_steps": [
            "Phase 1: Data Acquisition – Collect historical stock prices, transaction data, and analyst notes from multiple exchanges. Establish a robust data pipeline using Spark for data ingestion and cleaning.",
            "Phase 2: Network Construction – Utilize Neo4j to construct a graph representing investor relationships, trading patterns, and correlation analysis. Define core nodes (investors) and edges (transactions, relationships).",
            "Phase 3: Node Importance Assessment – Employ a weighted scoring system based on network centrality measures (PageRank, Betweenness) and the student’s expertise in identifying influential individuals or groups.",
            "Phase 4: Sentiment Analysis – Integrate a Natural Language Processing (NLP) module (NLTK/Spacy) to analyze analyst sentiment and news feeds to gauge market sentiment towards specific entities/assets.",
            "Phase 5: Model Development – Develop a regression model (using Scikit-learn) to predict portfolio allocation based on the network analysis and sentiment scores. Evaluate model performance using appropriate metrics."
